title: Soft Date Tagine with Japanese Twist
description: A sweet and savory dessert inspired by Japanese and Moroccan flavors, featuring soft, Egyptian dates in a rich tagine sauce.
instructions: 2. In a small saucepan, combine tagine and sake. Heat over low heat, stirring occasionally, until the mixture is fragrant and slightly thickened.
3. Add the honey and butter to the saucepan. Stir until the butter has melted.
4. Add the soft dates to the saucepan and cook for 2-3 minutes, or until they start to caramelize.
5. Add the Egyptian dates to the saucepan and cook for another 2 minutes, or until they are well coated with the tagine sauce.
6. Cut the mochi into small pieces and place them on a baking sheet.
7. Bake the mochi in the preheated oven for 10-12 minutes, or until they are lightly golden brown.
8. To assemble the dessert, place a few pieces of mochi on a plate and top with the date mixture.
